brief_justification: Paper A is a computer-vision inverse problem: it reconstructs a radius-indexed pairwise boundary-flow field from a single endpoint range-expansion image and statistically tests whether the pattern is transitive or cyclic (a non-transitive evolutionary game), via observability/stability proofs and Gaussian cyclicity testing. Paper B is spectral geometry, sharpening asymptotic constants in a Kuznetsov-type submanifold counting function for Laplace-Beltrami eigenvalues under a measure-zero looping-times condition. There is no role-for-role correspondence between tomographic flow-field recovery plus cyclicity hypothesis testing and eigenvalue-counting asymptotics; superficial hints like 'cyclic residuals' vs 'looping times' denote unrelated concepts, so no shared machinery holds.
